comment: Canonical home of the LabVIEW CI tooling -- the SINGLE place to redirect existing clients and new installs to a different repository for UPDATES and INSTALLATION. How it works: every client reads this file from the repo it currently pulls from (raw.githubusercontent.com/<that repo>/<ref>/.github/labview-ci/source.json); if "repo" below names a DIFFERENT repository than the one it was read from, the tooling has relocated, so the update-check, the one-click 'Update now' upgrade, the web installer, and the curl/irm CLI installers all follow it there -- and after one redirected update/install each client re-pins its own .github/labview-ci.yml to the new repo, so it polls the new home directly from then on. TODAY this names this very repository, so there is no redirect (clients keep using their current source). TO RELOCATE (e.g. when this project moves to an official NI repo): (1) in the NEW repo, set "repo" to the NEW repo's OWN owner/name so its own clients do not bounce; (2) in THIS repo, set "repo" to the NEW repo's owner/name; (3) bump the catalog version (.github/labview-ci/catalog.json) so clients see the move in the What's New dialog. Keep this repo reachable afterwards so already-installed clients can follow the pointer on their next update. NOTE: only clients running the version that introduced this pointer (or newer) will auto-follow; older clients adopt the follow-logic the next time they update.
